data.body: ENGINE ENHANCEMENT PULLED INTO SCOPE + OSS CACHING RESEARCH FOLDED IN. Caching now spans the shared runner seam AND the engine RunnerAgentStep (workflow/runs.py) so all workflow agent steps + other workflows cache, not just plan-review. OSS precedent CONFIRMS the CachePoint-in-user-content + anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions approach (Arize/phoenix, vitali87/code-graph-rag, shotgun-sh, josca42/varro). CRITICAL gotcha added: caching ⨯ parallelization — a naive concurrent fan-out makes all ~16 calls MISS+WRITE (≈20x input cost); fix is WARM-THEN-FAN-OUT (one call warms, await first token, then parallelize ⇒ ≈2.75x). TTL stays 5m under warming (reads refresh the window free). Other constraints folded: byte-stable prefix, 3/4 breakpoint budget, anthropic-only gating for cache_* settings, 4096-token Opus floor, 20-block lookback, OpenRouter drops CachePoint. Watch pydantic-ai #3453/#3722. Verify structured-output interaction with a targeted test.
